notes: Captured the official COTO/SANRAL/Department of Transport geometric-design standard: TRH17 'Geometric Design of Rural Roads' (Pretoria 1988), the recognised South African geometric design guideline. Downloaded free from the official Department of Transport repository (transport.gov.za), which hosts COTO standards. No dedicated standalone 'COTO Geometric Design Manual' is freely posted on any official source (nra.co.za/SANRAL, transport.gov.za, coto.online): COTO's currently published standards are the 2020 Standard Specifications for Road and Bridge Works (construction, not geometry); TRH17 remains the official geometric-design guideline. SANRAL (nra.co.za) does not expose a public technical-document library; coto.online seed unreachable. Validated PDF: 120 pages, valid. UTG1-1986 (urban geometric design guidelines) is the companion urban guideline also available at transport.gov.za if needed.
